response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack TypeScript/Node.js engineer with broad experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems, but falls well short of the mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, with no indication of Windows-platform C++ development. There is no demonstrated fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain expertise, and his SQL experience (MySQL) does not address the required SQL Server or Oracle proficiency. His engagement at RIDE Capital touches financial services but was purely TypeScript/Node.js at a general retail-investment platform level, not capital-markets systems.
strengths: Strong CI/CD and DevOps background across multiple roles with Docker, GitLab, and multi-OS CI servers
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency systems at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Demonstrated financial-services exposure at RIDE Capital using rigorous engineering practices (TDD, trunk-based development)
concerns: C++ experience totals roughly six months (NI, 2014–2015) with no Windows-platform focus — far below the required 5–10 years
No fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital-markets domain knowledge; primary domain is web/direct-mail/GIS
SQL experience limited to MySQL; no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency required by the role

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth and Windows platform experience
No capital markets or fixed income domain expertise
